basicDetails.schemeName: Grant for Sex-Sorted Semen to Livestock Farmers

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "Grant for Sex-Sorted Semen to Livestock Farmers" was introduced by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote breed improvement and increase milk production by encouraging the use of sex-sorted semen for artificial insemination in cows and buffaloes.
schemeContent.benefits_md: Under this scheme, a grant of ₹400 per dose is provided to cattle farmers for breed improvement through artificial insemination of cows and buffaloes using sex-sorted semen.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote breed improvement and increase milk production by encouraging the use of sex-sorted semen for artificial insemination in cows and buffaloes.

children.text: The scheme "Grant for Sex-Sorted Semen to Livestock Farmers" was introduced by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote breed improvement and increase milk production by encouraging the use of sex-sorted semen for artificial insemination in cows and buffaloes.
Under this scheme, a grant of ₹400 per dose is provided to cattle farmers for breed improvement through artificial insemination of cows and buffaloes using sex-sorted semen.
After contacting the nearest veterinary hospital, animal service center, or UPSA center, artificial insemination is carried out. The cattle rearer is not required to submit any documents.

applicationProcess.process_md: After contacting the nearest veterinary hospital, animal service center, or UPSA center, artificial insemination is carried out. The cattle rearer is not required to submit any documents.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand. 1. The applicant must be engaged in the rearing of cows and/or buffaloes.

children.text: The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
The applicant must be engaged in the rearing of cows and/or buffaloes.
